---
title: "The Fifty World-Running Words"
---

| Sigil | Term                 | Cluster                                             | Gloss                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| ----- | -------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 0001  | abstraction          | Boundaries, seams, and collaboration                | A deliberate forgetting of detail so larger structure can be reasoned about without drowning in mechanism. Shadow: hiding the only failure that matters.                                |
| 0002  | adapter              | Boundaries, seams, and collaboration                | A negotiated translator between unlike shapes. Use it when two systems must meet without infecting one another. Shadow: adapter jungles that preserve confusion rather than resolve it. |
| 0006  | API gateway          | Boundaries, seams, and collaboration                | A threshold that centralizes entry, policy, and routing at the edge of a service world. Shadow: a convenience chokepoint that quietly becomes a monolith.                               |
| 0012  | boundary             | Boundaries, seams, and collaboration                | The line that says where one responsibility ends and another begins. Shadow: porous edges that smear blame, ownership, and test scope.                                                  |
| 0013  | bounded context      | Boundaries, seams, and collaboration                | A protection against semantic bleed: the same word may not mean the same thing in every subsystem. Shadow: false unification through shared names.                                      |
| 0030  | coupling             | Boundaries, seams, and collaboration                | The hidden tax paid when change in one place forces change elsewhere. Name it early if you want modularity to survive contact with reality.                                             |
| 0034  | dependency inversion | Boundaries, seams, and collaboration                | Stable policy should not kneel before unstable detail. Shadow: concrete dependencies hard-wired into places that should remain portable.                                                |
| 0055  | interface            | Boundaries, seams, and collaboration                | A disciplined seam that lets unlike bodies cooperate without swapping internal organs. Shadow: tight coupling and type bleed.                                                           |
| 0347  | schema               | Boundaries, seams, and collaboration                | The declared shape of stored meaning. Shadow: drift, silent coercion, and backward-incompatible surprise.                                                                               |
| 0576  | retry                | Runtime pressure, behavior, and operational physics | Failure does not necessarily end the ritual; policy may permit another attempt. Shadow: blind repetition against a broken precondition.                                                 |
| 0598  | timeout              | Runtime pressure, behavior, and operational physics | Waiting is not free and patience must have a boundary. A timeout is a contract with reality about how long uncertainty is allowed to persist.                                           |
| 0615  | cache                | Runtime pressure, behavior, and operational physics | Keep likely things close so future cost falls. Shadow: stale truth delivered at machine speed.                                                                                          |
| 0684  | zero-copy            | Runtime pressure, behavior, and operational physics | Data movement stripped down to the minimum number of duplications. Use it when the bottleneck is physical movement rather than arithmetic.                                              |
| 0778  | backpressure         | Runtime pressure, behavior, and operational physics | Resistance pushed upstream when demand outruns capacity. Without it, overload spreads faster than truth.                                                                                |
| 0805  | eventual consistency | State, durable truth, and reversibility             | Agreement postponed rather than denied. Shadow: stale reads treated as if they were fresh truth.                                                                                        |
| 0825  | latency              | Runtime pressure, behavior, and operational physics | The delay before useful work reaches the caller. Latency is what users feel even when throughput graphs look flattering.                                                                |
| 0848  | quorum               | State, durable truth, and reversibility             | Not everyone must agree, but enough must. This word turns distributed action into a threshold question instead of a unanimous fantasy.                                                  |
| 0898  | commit               | State, durable truth, and reversibility             | The moment a proposed change becomes durable history. Shadow: writing too early, before the world is actually ready to carry the new truth.                                             |
| 0926  | migration            | State, durable truth, and reversibility             | Stored reality changing shape without being lost. Good migrations are staged, inspectable, and reversible long enough to discover what the data actually contains.                      |
| 0943  | rollback             | State, durable truth, and reversibility             | Permission to retreat toward a previously trusted state. A system without rollback mistakes hope for safety.                                                                            |
| 0974  | attestation          | Observation, release, and proof surfaces            | A proof that a thing is what it claims to be or was produced the way it claims. It matters most when trust cannot be assumed.                                                           |
| 0976  | authenticate         | Authority, trust, and named shadows                 | Prove who is making the claim before deciding what the claimant may do. Shadow: trusting names that have not earned entrance.                                                           |
| 0978  | authorization        | Authority, trust, and named shadows                 | After identity is known, what acts are permitted? Shadow: accidental omnipotence hidden behind a successful login.                                                                      |
| 1007  | hash                 | Authority, trust, and named shadows                 | Arbitrary input reduced to a fixed fingerprint. Use it for identity, integrity, bucketing, and change detection; never mistake it for secrecy.                                          |
| 1017  | least privilege      | Authority, trust, and named shadows                 | Give only the authority required for the present act, no more. It is one of the simplest words for reducing future regret.                                                              |
| 1028  | policy               | Authority, trust, and named shadows                 | The declared rule by which a class of cases is decided. Good policy reduces arbitrary judgment; bad policy merely freezes confusion.                                                    |
| 1050  | signature            | Authority, trust, and named shadows                 | Identity or intent made checkable through cryptographic proof. Useful wherever trust must survive distance and replay.                                                                  |
| 1073  | canary               | Observation, release, and proof surfaces            | A deliberately limited release whose job is to discover whether production reality agrees with the story told in staging.                                                               |
| 1086  | deploy               | Observation, release, and proof surfaces            | The crossing from forge to world. Many good ideas become bad facts only after they are actually shipped.                                                                                |
| 1088  | diff                 | Observation, release, and proof surfaces            | A visible account of what changed between one state and another. Good diffs make review local; bad diffs turn truth into fog.                                                           |
| 1146  | benchmark            | Observation, release, and proof surfaces            | A performance claim forced to meet measurement. Benchmarks are where speed rhetoric either cashes out or dies.                                                                          |
| 1152  | contract test        | Boundaries, seams, and collaboration                | A promise checked at the seam between systems. Use it when mocks are too flattering and end-to-end tests are too blunt.                                                                 |
| 1172  | invariant            | State, durable truth, and reversibility             | A law that must remain true while the rest of the system is allowed to move. If you cannot say the invariant, you probably do not yet understand the change.                            |
| 1180  | observability        | Observation, release, and proof surfaces            | Enough internal evidence exists that you can infer what the system is doing from the outside. Not logs alone: legible state under pressure.                                             |
| 1186  | property-based test  | Observation, release, and proof surfaces            | Instead of checking one remembered example, ask whether a law survives many generated cases. Use it when you care more about the invariant than the anecdote.                           |
| 1202  | telemetry            | Observation, release, and proof surfaces            | Measurements leaving the system in a form other systems can compare, store, and alert on. Shadow: metric exhaust without explanatory power.                                             |
| 1204  | trace                | Observation, release, and proof surfaces            | A preserved path through execution. Use it when the question is not only what failed, but where the causal thread first bent.                                                           |
| 1281  | throughput           | Runtime pressure, behavior, and operational physics | How much useful work the system can push through a narrow world in bounded time. Shadow: chasing bulk capacity while tail latency burns the user.                                       |
| 1425  | atomic               | State, durable truth, and reversibility             | No halfway state is allowed to leak across the line. Use it when partial success would be indistinguishable from corruption.                                                            |
| 1445  | deterministic        | State, durable truth, and reversibility             | Given the same inputs, the same result should return. This is the antidote to drift, heisenbugs, and unreviewable behavior.                                                             |
| 1461  | idempotent           | State, durable truth, and reversibility             | The word that makes retries survivable: same request, same lasting effect. Shadow: duplicate charges, duplicate emails, and fear of recovery.                                           |
| 1462  | immutable            | Runtime pressure, behavior, and operational physics | Once written, this thing does not change in place. Immutability buys clarity, replayability, and safer sharing at the cost of update convenience.                                       |
| 1467  | linearizable         | Observation, release, and proof surfaces            | Every operation behaves as though it took effect at one real instant visible to all observers. Use it rarely, but mean it fully.                                                        |
| 1472  | memory-safe          | Runtime pressure, behavior, and operational physics | A promise that the system will not casually trespass across allocation boundaries. Shadow: leaks, corruption, and security bugs born from illegal touch.                                |
| 1481  | pure                 | Runtime pressure, behavior, and operational physics | Same inputs, same output, no hidden state smuggled in from the side. Use it to shrink reasoning scope.                                                                                  |
| 1492  | serializable         | State, durable truth, and reversibility             | Concurrency forced to behave as though transactions happened in one clean order. Expensive when overused, indispensable when history must remain sane.                                  |
| 1546  | leak                 | Authority, trust, and named shadows                 | Something escapes the boundary that should have held it: memory, secrets, file descriptors, authority, abstraction, or time.                                                            |
| 1561  | race                 | Authority, trust, and named shadows                 | An ordering curse: outcomes depend on timing you do not actually control. Name the race and the system becomes debuggable again.                                                        |
| 1564  | retry storm          | Authority, trust, and named shadows                 | Recovery logic amplifying failure instead of containing it. Unguided retries can turn a partial outage into a self-made siege.                                                          |
| 1567  | schema drift         | Authority, trust, and named shadows                 | The stored world and the assumed world have silently stopped matching. This is how migrations keep hurting after everyone thinks they are done.                                         |
